{"decision": "fail", "risk": "medium", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"], "percent_complete": 75, "value": 0.208, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.85, "event_time": 0, "rate": 0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Drain slope is less than the required 1/4 inch per foot, risking solids deposition and clogging."], "actions": ["Re-pitch the affected drain line to achieve a minimum slope of 1/4 inch per foot.", "Verify slope with a level or measuring tape along the entire run before closing up."], "rationale": "IPC 704 requires horizontal drainage piping to be run with a slope of not less than 1/4 inch per foot toward the point of disposal. A slope under this threshold is non-compliant and can cause solids to settle and strand, leading to blockages. The rough-complete stage is the right time to correct pitch before walls or ceilings are closed.", "references": ["IPC 704"]}
